question_or_choice: How should Delta describe a domain scholar developing research software without prior formal Python proficiency?

ai_assistance.summary: AI agents helped distinguish a bounded development method from a generalized claim that programming expertise is unnecessary.

alternatives.option: Mention AI only in a generic disclosure.
alternatives.reason: This would hide the scholar-led method and the developer's actual starting position.

alternatives.option: Make vibe coding the main paper contribution.
alternatives.reason: It would displace the tool-first stylometry contribution.

alternatives.option: Define scholarly vibe coding as a secondary, evidence-gated development case.
alternatives.reason: It preserves human scientific ownership while documenting AI implementation support.

accepted_option: Define scholarly vibe coding as a secondary, scholar-led and evidence-gated development method.
rationale: The account is faithful to the development process without claiming that AI replaces programming expertise or that the model transfers to every scholar.
evidence_required: PromptEvent and Ticket coverage from P001 onward
HumanDecision records for method, claim, and acceptance choices
Failure and correction records
P015 provenance coverage audit
source_quote: Python öğrenmeden de var; sonuçta ben de tam olarak bilmiyorum, kendi önerdiğimiz scholarly vibe coding ile geliştiriyoruz.
provenance_notes: Transcribed from the current Codex thread. The interface did not expose a message-level UTC timestamp, so the ledger uses the P001 capture time and records the limitation.

question_or_choice: May Delta say that users can begin supported workflows without first learning R or Python?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex separated a structural no-prior-coding statement from unverified ease, learnability, or methodological competence claims.

alternatives.option: Ban all wording about learning R or Python.
alternatives.reason: It erases an intended product value and the developer's own experience.

alternatives.option: Claim the tool is easy for everyone.
alternatives.reason: No participant study supports general ease or learnability.

alternatives.option: Allow a bounded no-prior-coding statement while retaining method responsibility.
alternatives.reason: The interface can remove a coding prerequisite without proving general usability.

accepted_option: State that supported workflows require neither prior R/Python coding nor user-authored R/Python code, without claiming universal ease or method-free use.
rationale: This describes the technical prerequisite honestly while preserving the distinction between operating code and interpreting stylometric evidence.
evidence_required: CE-01 browser E2E
Prerequisite documentation audit
Claim-lint report
source_quote: Python öğrenmeden de var; sonuçta ben de tam olarak bilmiyorum, kendi önerdiğimiz scholarly vibe coding ile geliştiriyoruz.
provenance_notes: Transcribed from the current Codex thread and linked to the bounded wording in ADR-0008.

alternatives.option: Document FAIR only when the public release is prepared.
alternatives.reason: Late documentation cannot reliably reconstruct decisions, failures, or scope changes.

alternatives.option: Publish source code without structured development provenance.
alternatives.reason: Source availability alone does not make decisions, claims, tests, or limitations transparent.

alternatives.option: Record FAIR-oriented provenance and evidence at every acceptance-gated stage.
alternatives.reason: This preserves findable, machine-readable, inspectable links among human decisions, AI assistance, code, tests, failures, and claims.

accepted_option: Make FAIR-oriented transparency continuous from each ticket opening through acceptance and handoff.

question_or_choice: How should P003 use client-declared MIME when explicit upload role, extension, and role-specific content validation are already required?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex and an independent FAIR reviewer distinguished untrusted client MIME metadata from authoritative content parsing and identified an ASCII PDF-signature gap in the text parser.

alternatives.option: Reject every upload that omits a MIME declaration.
alternatives.reason: Client MIME is optional and untrusted; requiring its presence would not establish content type and would reject otherwise inspectable inputs.

alternatives.option: Ignore MIME even when the client supplies it.
alternatives.reason: A supplied contradiction between role and MIME is useful fail-closed evidence and should not be discarded.

alternatives.option: Require explicit role and extension, require supplied MIME to agree, and make the role-specific content parser authoritative even when MIME is absent.
alternatives.reason: This preserves deterministic fail-closed disagreement checks without mistaking client metadata for a security boundary.

accepted_option: Treat MIME as untrusted advisory metadata: when supplied it must agree with the explicit role; absence is allowed only because the role-specific content parser remains mandatory and authoritative.
rationale: The policy makes the acceptance criterion testable and honest. Known binary/document signatures are rejected by content regardless of MIME, while MIME absence does not become a type claim.
evidence_required: P003 parser report
MIME disagreement and MIME-absent positive tests
PDF/RTF and BOM-prefixed document-signature negative fixtures
Updated P003 AC-01 wording
provenance_notes: The user's native acceptance of the audit recommendations authorized the bounded corrective implementation. This record captures the technical choice exposed by independent review; it is not final acceptance of the whole P003 ticket.

question_or_choice: Where should Delta's canonical working copy and remote Git backup live after Google Drive synchronization made the existing development folder impractical?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex compared keeping the repository in Drive, moving it to Desktop, and using a dedicated local Developer directory backed by a private GitHub repository; it then executed the authorized guarded migration.

alternatives.option: Keep active development inside Google Drive.
alternatives.reason: Repository-local environments and caches caused repeated synchronization of thousands of generated files and disrupted Drive use.

alternatives.option: Move the active repository to Desktop without a remote.
alternatives.reason: Desktop would remove Drive synchronization but would not provide a durable remote Git history or a stable development root.

alternatives.option: Use ~/Developer/delta.lemmata_app as the canonical working copy with a private oguzkoran-max GitHub origin.
alternatives.reason: It separates generated development files from Drive while preserving tracked history remotely and keeping future public release decisions separate.

accepted_option: Move active development to ~/Developer/delta.lemmata_app, create a private oguzkoran-max/delta.lemmata.app GitHub repository, verify a clean clone, and retain the old Drive folder in Trash until the user chooses to empty it.
rationale: The guarded sequence removes the synchronization bottleneck without conflating a private backup with public FAIR release and without changing P003 acceptance status.
evidence_required: PRIVATE visibility and main default-branch check
Remote branch and tag verification
Clean GitHub clone at the active commit
Bootstrap and full verification in the new path
Byte-equivalent preservation of ignored P001 generated evidence
Old Drive path absent and recoverable Trash copy present

question_or_choice: Which metadata interaction baseline should P004 use, and should Corpus Review include informative visualizations?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex presented ten beginner-oriented product choices with one explicit recommendation and rationale for each, then separated corpus-description graphics from later scientific-result graphics.

alternatives.option: Use CSV-only metadata, expose every field at once, and provide a table-only review.
alternatives.reason: This is efficient for experts but creates an unnecessary entry barrier and makes corpus imbalances, chronology, and rights restrictions difficult to scan.

alternatives.option: Use only a guided form and decorative charts.
alternatives.reason: This does not scale to research corpora and could make aesthetics appear to substitute for metadata evidence.

alternatives.option: Use a form plus versioned CSV, progressive disclosure, one independent work per TXT, exact confirmed mapping, uncertainty-aware dates, separate rights actions, tiered validation, and an accessible visual Corpus Review.
alternatives.reason: This supports beginners and experienced researchers while preserving deterministic mapping, epistemic caution, bulk workflows, and visible corpus limitations.

accepted_option: Adopt all ten recommended P004 metadata UX defaults and include beautiful but evidence-bound corpus-description visuals: a work timeline, composition bars, rights action matrix, metadata completeness matrix, and readiness summary.
rationale: The hybrid design removes R/Python and CSV-only barriers without hiding scholarly responsibility. The visuals help users understand what their corpus contains and what remains unresolved, while scientific-result graphics remain gated behind the real analysis and benchmark Tickets.
evidence_required: Versioned form and CSV round-trip tests
Exact file-to-work mapping tests
Chronology and rights negative fixtures
Corpus Review desktop and mobile screenshots
Chart text-equivalent and non-color accessibility checks
No-placeholder-scientific-result audit

provenance_notes: The first sentence explicitly accepts the ten immediately preceding recommended defaults. The second sentence is recorded as a requirement for informative visual design, not as authorization to fabricate or prematurely display stylometric results.

question_or_choice: How should the Corpus workflow, metadata field model, rights uncertainty, teaching layers, and implementation order be finalized before P004 coding begins?

alternatives.reason: This would exclude legitimate offline sources and burden simple proximity workflows with fields that only another purpose needs.

alternatives.reason: This keeps the workbench approachable while preserving deterministic validation, FAIR-oriented evidence, explicit uncertainty, and purpose-specific methodological responsibility.

accepted_option: Adopt the recommended second- and third-round defaults and consolidate all three rounds into one P004 design and implementation contract.
rationale: The resulting design gives beginners a guided route and researchers a versioned bulk route, keeps identity and rights universally mandatory, makes chronology purpose-specific, exposes confounds without silently inventing data, and prevents UI work from preceding the canonical data model and validators.

question_or_choice: Should P005 AC-08 continue waiting for GitHub's transient artifact-storage quota, rely only on job logs, or preserve the exact Linux CI supply-chain package permanently in Git with checksum and privacy validation?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex investigated repeated upload-artifact quota failures, confirmed that Linux verification, evidence generation, and the canonical container already passed, compared quota retry, log-only, and Git-backed evidence channels, and recommended a branch-scoped one-time Linux capture with path-neutral validation and permanent checksums.

alternatives.option: Keep waiting for GitHub artifact-storage recalculation and retry the same upload-artifact step.
alternatives.reason: Four attempts passed every technical gate but failed at the account-level storage boundary, leaving P005 completion dependent on an opaque external counter.

alternatives.option: Treat successful job logs as sufficient and close AC-08 without retaining the generated files.
alternatives.reason: Logs show generation but do not preserve the exact SBOM, audit, environment, and checksum bytes for later inspection or rerun.

alternatives.option: Use a temporary branch-scoped Linux workflow to validate the exact eight-file package, commit it with an outer SHA-256 manifest, then remove the write-capable workflow after capture.
alternatives.reason: This keeps the hard evidence gate, removes the quota dependency, preserves inspectable FAIR-oriented evidence in Git history, and limits write permission to a disposable branch workflow.

accepted_option: Preserve P005 AC-08 supply-chain evidence as an exact Linux CI-generated, path-neutral, eight-file Git package with nested source checksums, an outer SHA-256 manifest, privacy validation, and reciprocal Run/Ticket links; remove the temporary capture workflow after success.
rationale: The P005 criterion requires Linux CI, SBOM, audit, and container evidence but does not require GitHub's transient artifact service. A permanent checksum-bound package is more durable and auditable while remaining small and free of corpus content or local paths.
evidence_required: Exact-source Ubuntu 24.04 workflow run and job identifiers
Eight expected non-empty UTF-8 evidence files
Machine validation of JSON roots, source checksum inventory, regular files, and private-path absence
Outer SHA-256 manifest verified against the committed package
Green regular Linux verify and canonical container jobs
Reciprocal HumanDecision, Run, Ticket, ADR, and commit links
provenance_notes: The short native continuation immediately followed one explicit binary choice between waiting for GitHub artifact storage and the recommended Git-backed Linux evidence path. It is recorded as contextual assent to that bounded evidence-channel decision. It authorizes implementation and verification only; it is not P005 product-owner acceptance, a scientific result, deployment approval, or final release acceptance.

question_or_choice: Should P006 adopt all seven recommended method defaults for its synthetic fixture oracle, locked environment, numeric tolerances, metric roles, known-only fitting, and bounded claim language?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex translated the architecture audit into seven beginner-oriented choices: project-authored CC0 fixtures; R 4.5.2 and stylo 0.7.71 in canonical Linux with C.UTF-8, UTC, NFC, and seed 20260713; exact feature equality; 1e-6 matrix and 1e-12 structural/tie tolerances; Classic Delta primary with Eder and Cosine sensitivity results; known-only fitting; and fixture-local claims only.

alternatives.option: Accept all seven recommended P006 method defaults as one bounded protocol.
alternatives.reason: The owner instructed Codex to continue immediately after the complete package and the explicit acceptance instruction were presented.

alternatives.option: Revise one or more fixture, environment, tolerance, metric, leakage, or claim defaults before implementation.
alternatives.reason: The owner supplied no revision and instead authorized continuation in the immediately preceding decision context.

alternatives.option: Continue implementation without freezing human-owned method choices.
alternatives.reason: That would let an AI agent silently determine the benchmark and scientific acceptance conditions.

accepted_option: Use project-authored CC0 synthetic fixtures; canonical Linux with R 4.5.2, stylo 0.7.71, C.UTF-8, UTC, NFC, and seed 20260713; exact ordered features; matrix tolerance 1e-6; structural and tie tolerance 1e-12; Classic Delta primary with Eder and Cosine as separate sensitivity metrics; known-only fitting; and fixture-local, version-specific claims.
rationale: The protocol is redistributable, deterministic, compatible with the inspected stylo version, resistant to unknown-data leakage, and narrow enough to avoid turning a fixture parity test into an authorship, reliability, preprocessing, deployment, or general reproducibility claim.
evidence_required: CC0 license and hashes for every project-authored synthetic fixture
Locked canonical Linux environment and complete R session information
Checksum-frozen independent direct-stylo oracle generated before worker comparison
Exact feature-inventory and tolerance-bounded matrix parity reports
Unknown-canary invariance for every known-derived fitting artifact and known-known distance
Claim-matrix wording that keeps full CE-04, full CE-07, public analysis, benchmarks, FAIR package, Pinocchio, and deployment in later tickets
provenance_notes: The user did not repeat the proposed acceptance sentence verbatim. The imperative is treated as contextual assent because it immediately followed one explicit seven-item recommendation and a request either to accept it or name revisions. This decision authorizes the bounded P006 method and implementation sequence; it is not a scientific result, P006 acceptance, public Run activation, deployment approval, or final release acceptance.

alternatives.option: Revise one or more transformation, threshold, severity, materialization, admission, output, or wording choices before coding.

alternatives.option: Implement without a human-owned method decision.
alternatives.reason: That would make AI-generated preprocessing and corpus-health policy appear to be an unowned scientific standard.

accepted_option: Accept delta-surface-words-v1; exact-token candidate-only exclusions; the separate P007 annotation overlay; independent-work counting by parent work; blocker, strong_warning, and note severities; the declared exact/near/shared-passage, 4:1 length, 3:1 group, six-work, three-chronology-point, and two-known-work gates; P005-owned private materialization; one expiring one-time hash-bound READY receipt; content-free outputs; and the Upload, Describe, Prepare, Health review English UX.
rationale: The package is deterministic, inspectable, compatible with frozen P003-P006 contracts, conservative about work independence and corpus warnings, and explicit that automation does not certify corpus quality or prove authorship, causality, representativeness, or publication readiness.

Golden Unicode and deterministic-preparation fixtures
Boundary fixtures for every accepted diagnostic threshold

question_or_choice: Should Delta keep the original late-2026 public-beta sequence, activate an unqualified site immediately, or target a strictly bounded public alpha within 72 hours and an evidence-dependent manuscript draft during August 2026?

ai_assistance.summary: Codex separated a usable public alpha from scientific release, identified the minimum P007-P009 and P014 safety slice required before exposure, and retained benchmarks, calibration, full FAIR rerun, Pinocchio, expert walkthrough, and final claims as later gates.

alternatives.option: Activate the current incomplete interface immediately as if the tool were finished.
alternatives.reason: The current build cannot yet prepare real text, run the public workflow, explain results, or prove deployment isolation.

alternatives.option: Keep all public access blocked until the original November public-beta milestone.
alternatives.reason: It delays useful feedback even though a clearly labeled, constrained alpha can be made safe sooner.

alternatives.option: Target public alpha by 2026-07-17 only after a minimal end-to-end and deployment safety gate, while retaining later scientific-release gates and preparing the full manuscript draft in August only from completed evidence.
alternatives.reason: This meets the owner's speed priority without relabeling an alpha as a validated release or weakening scientific and security claims.

accepted_option: Target a clearly labeled Public alpha by 2026-07-17 after P007 preparation/health, a minimal P008 run path, basic P009 result guardrails, and the minimum P014 isolation/limit/rollback slice pass; continue P010-P013 and full P012/P014/P015 afterward; target a full manuscript draft in August 2026 only for claims supported by then-complete evidence.
rationale: A narrow alpha can expose the real tool quickly while preserving the distinction among functional availability, scientific validation, FAIR-oriented release, expert acceptance, and publication readiness. Missing gates reduce scope or delay activation; they are not waived by the date.
evidence_required: Real upload-to-result browser E2E for the exposed workflow
Visible Public alpha and experimental-scope wording
Strict file, token, queue, CPU, RAM, PID, timeout, rate, and concurrency limits
Separate Delta identity, container, port, environment, volume, and secret boundary
TLS, strict Host, egress policy, health check, rollback rehearsal, and Lemmata smoke test
Results paired with tables and What this does not establish guidance
No scientific-release, FAIR-certified, authorship-proof, usability-proven, or publication-ready wording
August manuscript claim inventory linked only to completed evidence
